empty.loadingBody: One moment while we fetch your plan.
empty.noneBody: Complete the interview and your roadmap will appear here.

emailCard.title: Don’t lose this roadmap
emailCard.body: We’ll email it to you — the link signs you in on any device, no password to invent. You’ll also get a short weekly roundup of what’s due while your move is underway.

banners.penaltyOne: 1 item carries a financial penalty if missed.
banners.penaltyMany: {{count}} items carry financial penalties if missed.

toolbar.exportA11y: Export your roadmap as a PDF

week.clearWaiting: Your remaining steps are waiting on earlier milestones — the full roadmap has the whole picture.

sheet.addDate: did it earlier? add the date
sheet.dateHint: When did you actually do this? Downstream dates re-flow from it.
sheet.dateUnreadable: I can’t read that yet — try the month as a word, e.g. "25 April 2026".
sheet.changeLink: Something changed? Tell Lola to update your plan →
sheet.overdueNudge: This one's past due. Already handled it? Mark it done — even with the real date — and the plan re-flows around what actually happened. Plans changed? Tell Lola below.
sheet.regionalBody: The specifics of this step are set by each comunidad autónoma. The source link covers the national rules; your region's own rates and windows apply.
sheet.regionalBodyWithRegion: The specifics of this step are set by each comunidad autónoma — yours: {{region}}. The source link covers the national rules; your region's own rates and windows apply.

sample.name: Susan & Tom
sample.blurb: retired couple from Austin, moving on the non-lucrative visa with their dog, still choosing between Valencia and Málaga — and hoping to become citizens one day
sample.title: This is {{name}}'s plan.
sample.body: {{name}} — {{blurb}}. Get Camino turned their situation into the {{count}}-step, deadline-aware roadmap below. Yours will look different: a few answers about your own move, and the same engine builds it just for you.
sample.ctaNote: Free · about 3 minutes · no account needed to start
sample.tease: In your own plan, Lola coaches you through this step, you check it off, and the dates re-flow around what actually happens.
sample.closingBody: Different passport, work, family, or plans — different roadmap. Tell Lola about your move and get the version that's actually yours, with real deadlines in the right order.

retirees.tag: US retirees on the non-lucrative visa

eu-family.name: Anna & Max
eu-family.tag: an EU family — no visa needed
eu-family.blurb: a German couple with two kids, moving to Valencia on EU freedom of movement — no visa cluster at all, but school enrollment, registration, and healthcare still have an order

digital-nomad.name: Maya
digital-nomad.tag: a solo remote worker on the digital-nomad visa
digital-nomad.blurb: a Canadian software developer keeping her remote job, heading for Barcelona on the digital-nomad visa

property-owners.name: James & Priya
property-owners.tag: UK owners of a Spanish home
property-owners.blurb: a British couple who already bought a house in Andalucía — the notary, registry, and tax chain is real, and their UK licences are on a six-month clock

severityBlurb.penalty: Missing this can trigger a financial penalty.
severityBlurb.required: A legal requirement for your situation.
severityBlurb.recommended: Strongly advised, though not strictly mandatory.
severityBlurb.info: Informational — a milestone to be aware of.

sourceBlurb.official: Verified against an official government source (AEAT, extranjería, BOE).
sourceBlurb.recommendation: Get Camino’s practical recommendation — not a legal requirement. How strongly we suggest it is shown by its priority; confirm the specifics for your own case.

timing.recurringDetail: This repeats every year for as long as it applies to you.
timing.pendingDetail: This can't be given a date until {{event}}.
timing.estimatedDetail: This date is an estimate based on typical timelines — give Lola firm dates to sharpen it.
timing.firmDetail: A firm date based on what you told Lola.

diff.none: Nothing in your plan needed to change.

coach.error: Sorry — I couldn't load that just now. Try asking again in a moment.

hints.visa: e.g. I got a remote job, so we’re switching to the digital-nomad visa.
hints.residency: e.g. My residence card (TIE) was issued on 2027-02-01.
hints.tax: e.g. I’ll be freelancing in Spain after all, not just living off savings.
hints.health: e.g. We’re only staying six months, not moving permanently.
hints.mobility: e.g. We decided no one will drive in Spain.
hints.banking: e.g. Our arrival moved two months later.
hints.family: e.g. We got married last month.
hints.property: e.g. We decided to rent instead of buy.
hints.admin: e.g. We’re arriving in November instead of September.
hints.generic: e.g. Our arrival date moved to next spring.

changeNote.errBody: I couldn’t process that just now — please try again.
changeNote.noChangeBody: Nothing in your plan needed to change. If a step happened (or will happen) on a specific date, mark it done "on a date" and the plan re-flows from that.
changeNote.noopBody: I’ve saved that, and nothing in your plan needed to move because of it.
changeNote.remodelTitle: That was useful — I’ve remodelled your plan!

share.a11y: Share a read-only link to your roadmap
share.title: Share your roadmap
share.body: Anyone with the link sees a read-only copy — your steps and dates, exactly as the engine builds them. They can’t edit anything, and no account is needed.
share.caveat: The link itself encodes your answers (nationalities, dates, plans — never your notes). Share it only with people you’d tell those things to.

shared.title: Someone’s road to Spain
shared.body: {{count}} steps, built from their answers by the same engine that would build yours — real requirements, real order, official sources on every rule.
shared.metaDescription: A read-only Get Camino roadmap, shared by its owner.
shared.invalidBody: Links can get truncated in transit — ask the sender to copy it again. Or see what your own roadmap would look like; it takes about three minutes.

regionalFacts.itp_flat: In {{region}}, the general transfer-tax (ITP) rate on a resale home is {{rate}} of the price — reduced rates exist for some buyers and price bands.
regionalFacts.itp_brackets: In {{region}}, ITP on a resale home is progressive: {{low}} up to {{cap}}, rising in brackets to {{high}} at the top.
regionalFacts.wealth_relief: {{region}} applies a {{pct}} regional relief on this tax — in practice most residents pay none, though the state solidarity tax can still apply above {{threshold}}.
regionalFacts.wealth_scale: In {{region}}, the wealth tax starts above a {{exempt}} personal exemption (plus the primary-home allowance), with rates rising to {{top}} at the top.
